purpose: Lock the known sarf hover regressions as executable assertions. Each row pins a norm-collapsed key, the wrong gloss that shipped (or would ship if norm() decided), and the correct gloss OR an explicit 'pending' when the distinguishing diacritic is dropped by the key. A resolver passes the suite only if it produces correct_gloss_or_pending for every key. Companion to ../examples/qamus-regressions.jsonl (narrative form) — this file is the pass/fail contract.

assertion_schema.key: the norm/norm_strict key the resolver receives (over-collapsed)
assertion_schema.surfaces: the distinct vowelled surfaces that share the key
assertion_schema.wrong_gloss: what a surface/norm-keyed resolver emits incorrectly
assertion_schema.correct_gloss_or_pending: the defensible gloss, or 'pending' when the key cannot decide
assertion_schema.rule: the operational test the resolver must apply (which signal, which letter)

pending_contract: 'pending' is a PASS state, not a failure: when the key drops shadda/voice/hamza-seat/content-harakah, the only correct output is pending with the matching reason from ../../qamus/schemas/authored-gloss.schema.json (multi_sense_root | derived_form_needs_review | hamza_sensitive_homograph). Emitting the commoner reading instead is the failure.

assertions.wrong_gloss: descended (collapsing all three onto Form I)
assertions.rule: Form II نَزَّلَ 'sent down gradually' carries a shadda on the zāy; Form I نَزَلَ 'descended' does not; Form IV أَنْزَلَ adds an initial hamza. The key drops shadda and hamza-seat, so it cannot separate the three forms of one root — pending (derived_form_needs_review).

assertions.wrong_gloss: descended (Form I sense applied to Form II)
assertions.rule: When the shadda is preserved and certified, Form II نَزَّلَ takes its intensive/distributive 'sent down' gloss — distinct from Form I 'descended'. Two-vote because it is a derived-form sense shift.

Triple homograph on root م ل ك: مُلْك (ḍamma-sukūn) 'dominion/sovereignty', مَلِك (fatḥa-kasra) 'king', مَلَك (fatḥa-fatḥa) 'angel'. The key drops every harakah that separates them — decide on vowels + context, never on the key; absent the harakāt it is pending (multi_sense_root).
With the fatḥa-kasra vowels present and certified, مَلِك is unambiguously 'king'. Still two-vote because the sibling readings مُلْك/مَلَك make the surface high-risk.

Root أ م م: أُمّ 'mother' vs أُمَّة 'community/nation' — the tāʾ marbūṭa and the wider derivation distinguish them, but the bare key (which also brushes against أَمَّ 'led' and the preposition أَمَام) cannot decide. Pending until the surface + POS are pinned (multi_sense_root).
When the surface أُمَّة (shadda on mīm + tāʾ marbūṭa) is preserved and certified, gloss as 'community / nation', never 'mother'. The tāʾ marbūṭa is part of the lemma, not a suffix.

assertions.wrong_gloss: they were disbelieved (rare passive applied), or 'lied' (Form I applied to Form II)
assertions.rule: Three readings on root ك ذ ب: Form II كَذَّبَ 'denied/cried lies against' (shadda, dominant), Form I كَذَبَ 'lied', passive كُذِبَ 'was disbelieved' (rare, ḍamma-kasra). The key drops shadda AND voice — never auto-resolve; default-to-dominant must NOT fire on the rare reading (derived_form_needs_review).

With the shadda preserved and the active voice certified, Form II كَذَّبَ is the transitive 'denied / cried lies against (a sign, a messenger)' — not the intransitive Form I 'lied'. Two-vote for the derived-form sense shift.

assertions.wrong_gloss: brings out (Form IV applied to Form I), or 'goes out' (Form I applied to Form IV)
assertions.rule: Form I يَخْرُجُ (fatḥa-…-ḍamma) 'goes out' vs Form IV يُخْرِجُ (ḍamma-…-kasra) 'brings out'. The key erases the prefix and stem vowels that flip intransitive↔transitive — pending unless the harakāt are present (derived_form_needs_review).
With the ḍamma-prefix and kasra stem vowel certified, Form IV يُخْرِجُ is causative-transitive 'brings out / produces'. Two-vote for the derived-form sense shift from Form I.

عَلِيم is فَعِيل from root ع ل م 'All-Knowing'. The wrong gloss came from a data error filing it under أ ل م (أَلِيم 'painful'). Quarantine must cover the nominative عَلِيمٌ and accusative عَلِيمًا too, not only the bare عَلِيم. A surface-keyed authored gloss overrides the data-error spread. Two-vote because of the homograph proximity.

suite_invariants: Every assertion whose correct_gloss_or_pending == 'pending' MUST resolve to pending under the default (key-only) path — emitting any vowelled sibling's gloss is a regression.
Every '@vowelled' / '@form-aware' assertion is reachable ONLY when the named diacritic (shadda/voice/harakah/hamza-seat) is present in the source; if absent, the sibling 'pending' assertion governs.
No assertion may ship a public gloss except as {src:'qamus',kind:'authored',lang:'en'}; the internal data error (أ ل م misfile, dominant-sense pull) is evidence, never surfaced.
عَلِيمٌ (nominative) and عَلِيمًا (accusative) are the SAME assertion as عَلِيم — case-marking tanwīn must not split or re-expose the quarantine.
